verdict: PASS — all bias checks clear on cycle 2. Length-bias fix successful: correct answers now within 7–15% of mean answer length (well below 20% threshold). Positional distribution even. All 5 LOs covered. Short-answer q6 tests applied meaningful/nominal oversight distinction.

positional_bias.notes: Max share exactly 40% at position 0 — at the threshold but NOT exceeding it. PASS.

length_bias.notes: Cycle 2 fix: corpus section refs moved to feedback only; distractors lengthened to match correct answer footprint. 0/5 MCQs flagged.

distractor_plausibility.notes: All distractors capture plausible beginner misconceptions: confusing complexity with severity (q1), technical vs cognitive concerns (q2), nominal vs meaningful oversight (q3/q4), pilot overconfidence vs training gap (q5).

objective_coverage.notes: LO1→q1, LO2→q2, LO3→q3, LO4→q4, LO5→q5. q6 short-answer tests applied understanding of meaningful vs nominal oversight (LO2+LO3 applied).

beginner_fairness.notes: MCAS, EU AI Act, COMPAS all spelled out in stems or referenced in context. All questions within 9.11 scope. Distractors are beginner-accessible misconceptions.
